title: Creating a REST API with Math Operations

message.text:
create .Controller.MathController
make it a RESt api
add a method int add(int val1,int val2) and int sub(int a,int b)  add  return in response .logic only in controller layer and dont add logic in service layer .i want to call this api from browser guide me

editAgentRounds.reply: I'll add a new REST controller `MathController` with GET endpoints for add and sub, then run a quick error check. I'll create the new file at the absolute path in your workspace.

Done — I added `MathController` with two GET endpoints.

Checklist
- [x] Create `MathController` in `HelloWorldController` project
- [x] Implement add and sub methods (logic only in controller)
- [x] Verify no compile errors for the new file

What I changed
- Added file `src/main/java/com/example/demo/MathController.java` with:
  - GET /math/add?val1={int}&val2={int}  — returns val1 + val2
  - GET /math/sub?a={int}&b={int}        — returns a - b

How to run and call the API from your browser
1. Start the Spring Boot app from the `HelloWorldController` project directory.
   In Windows cmd.exe run:
   ```
   cd "C:\Users\dhana\OneDrive\Desktop\project workspace\HelloWorldController"
   .\mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
   ```
   Or run the `HelloWorldControllerApplication` main class from your IDE.

2. Open your browser and call the endpoints:
   - Add example:
     http://localhost:8080/math/add?val1=3&val2=4
     Response body: 7
   - Subtract example:
     http://localhost:8080/math/sub?a=10&b=6
     Response body: 4

Notes
- The controller returns plain JSON numeric responses (e.g., 7). Content-Type will be application/json.
- Endpoints expect integer query parameters; if you omit a required parameter or pass a non-integer, Spring will return a 400 Bad Request.
- Per your instruction, all logic is in the controller; no service layer changes were made.

If you'd like:
- I can add nicer JSON responses (e.g., { "result": 7 }) instead of a raw number.
- Add parameter validation and default values.
- Add POST endpoints that accept JSON payloads.
